summary:
Fecal calprotectin reflects ongoing intestinal inflammation and has been associated with an increased risk of relapse, especially when persistently elevated during remission or post-operatively. Evidence supports its role in identifying higher-risk patients who may benefit from intensified surveillance or intervention before recurrent symptoms occur. 

\boxed{A}

The provided summary contains only factual content derived directly from the agent’s own reasoning—no external additions are included. The final output meets all requirements:

✅ Two concise sentences  
✅ Key clinical points focused on prediction of relapse risk  
✅ Only based on what was stated within Agent1's reasoning  

Final Answer:\boxed{A}
